steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do you use to hear music?
questions.hint: On each side of your head they are.

questions.question_text: Which part of your face is above your lips?
What do you use to smile?
questions.hint: You show your teeth with it.

steps.title: My Happy Face
steps.text: This is my face. I have two big eyes. I have a small nose. My mouth is smiling. I am happy!

steps.audio_text: Hello! I am Tom. I have two blue eyes and a small nose. My mouth is laughing.

questions.question_text: I have two ___ to see with. (Vocabulary question)
I have one ___ . (Reading comprehension question)
How many mouths do you have?
True or False: Your face has a nose.

lessons.title: Listen and Look
lessons.topic: Auditory and decorative parts of the head

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do we use to listen to music?
questions.hint: We have one on each side of our head.

questions.question_text: What do we see in the mirror on our head?
questions.hint: It grows and you can brush it.

questions.question_text: What opens wide when we smile?
questions.hint: We use it to talk and eat.

steps.title: My Funny Face
steps.text: Look! This is my funny face. I have small ears and big blue hair. My head is round. Can you hear me with your ears?

steps.explanation: Say what color a part is.

steps.audio_text: Look at my head! I have two big ears and short hair. My eyes are small.

questions.question_text: Which word means the top part of your body? (Vocabulary question)
My ___ is brown. (Grammar question)
True or False: We see with our ears.
What do you use to listen? (Reading comprehension question)

lessons.title: My Whole Head
lessons.topic: Reviewing the head as a whole

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do you use to hear sounds?
questions.hint: They are on the side of your head.

questions.question_text: What lets you smell yummy cookies?
questions.hint: It's in the middle of your face.

questions.question_text: Which body part helps you talk and eat?

steps.title: Big Smile, Small Nose
steps.text: I have a big smile. This is my nose. It is small. The elephant has big ears. They are very large!

questions.question: Is my nose big or small?

steps.explanation: Use big or small to talk about size.

steps.audio_text: Hi there! Look, this is my brother. He has a big nose and tiny ears. His eyes are green!

questions.question: Are his ears big or tiny?

questions.question_text: I have a ___ smile. (Vocabulary question)
He ___ small ears. (Grammar question)
What part is used to hear sound? (Reading comprehension question)
What word describes something not big? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: 'This is my head.' is a complete sentence.

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What part of your body lets you see the stars at night?
questions.hint: You close them when you sleep!

questions.question_text: Which body part helps you hear a bird sing?
questions.hint: They are on the sides of your head.

questions.question_text: What part of your face do you use to wiggle and sniff?
questions.hint: It's in the middle of your face!

steps.title: Funny Face
steps.text: This is my funny face! I have two big blue eyes. I have one small orange nose. My mouth is big and smiles a lot. Do you like my face?

questions.question: How many noses does it have?

steps.audio_text: This is my playful dog. He has big, floppy ears and a cute, little nose. I love his wagging tail.

questions.question: What kind of ears does the dog have?
Does he have a wagging tail?

steps.prompt: Say: I have a big head and two small ears.
steps.expected_text: i have a big head and two small ears

questions.question_text: I ___ a big smile. (Grammar question)
Which part of your face helps you see? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: You use your ears to listen.
This is my ___ cat. (small, pink nose) (Reading comprehension question)
